name: MITRE.LIN.T1011.RULE Exfiltration Over Other Network Medium
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S sethostname -S setdomainname -F auid!=0 -k siem-network-modifications
-w /etc/hosts -p wa -k siem-network-modifications
-w /etc/sysconfig/network -p wa -k siem-network-modifications
-w /etc/network/ -p wa -k siem-network-modifications
-a always,exit -F dir=/etc/NetworkManager/ -F perm=wa -k siem-network-modifications
-w /etc/sysconfig/network -p wa -k siem-network-modifications

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-network-modifications
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when an event matches any of the following MITRE.LIN.T1016.RULE System Network Configuration Discovery
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1011.RULE Exfiltration Over Other Network Medium on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-network-modifications
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following MITRE.LIN.T1016.RULE System Network Configuration Discovery
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1011.RULE Exfiltration Over Other Network Medium

Event description: Adversaries could choose to do this if they have sufficient access or proximity, and the connection might not be secured or defended as well as the primary Internet-connected channel because it is not routed through the same enterprise network.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1011
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
